summary: Small documentation-only fix correcting a broken or stale source-code link in `docs/getting_started.md`. The linked Mewtwo SpecialHi example path changed from `ftMt_SpecialHi.c` to `ftMewtwo_SpecialHi.c`, while preserving the same commit hash and line range.
what_changed: No code, headers, build files, or matching logic were changed.
smart_moves: Kept the example anchored to a specific commit hash, preserving documentation stability even as the repository evolves.
Made the smallest possible edit: only the incorrect filename in the URL changed, reducing risk for a documentation PR.
Preserved the visible surrounding explanation and the referenced line range, indicating the intended example remained the same.
decomp_lessons: Getting-started documentation should use valid, stable links to real source files because newcomers rely on these examples to understand matching C code.
When files are renamed or naming conventions evolve, documentation links can silently become stale and should be updated alongside source tree changes.
Even tiny docs fixes are useful in decomp projects because broken onboarding references can slow down new contributors.
naming_conventions: The corrected file path uses `ftMewtwo_SpecialHi.c`, spelling out `Mewtwo` in the filename rather than the shorter `ftMt_SpecialHi.c` form.
The code excerpt still shows a symbol named `ftMt_SpecialHi_CreateGFX`, so this PR only evidences a filename/path convention update, not a function rename.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find other documentation links that reference renamed source files.
Search docs for `ftMt_SpecialHi.c` to ensure no stale references remain.
Check whether other fighter source paths use abbreviated versus full character names in filenames.
